71.reason: [Error: Cannot compare strings with != operator; use strcmp instead. Also, comparing to "\0" is incorrect as it's a null character, not a string.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: Cannot compare strings with != operator; use strcmp instead. Also, comparing to "\0" is incorrect as it's a null character, not a string.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: Comparing string with character literal using != operator. Should use strcmp instead.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  wrong pointer comparison; should use strcmp to check for empty string
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.2 , reason:  Pointer comparison with string literals is incorrect; should use strcmp.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  Logical error: Compares pointer addresses instead of string contents. Use 'words[i].word[0] != '\0'' or 'strlen(words[i].word) > 0' to check for non-empty words.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Error] Incorrect string comparison in C; != compares memory addresses, not string contents. Should use strcmp() or check words[i].word[0] != '\0'.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Comparing string literals with != is invalid in C; should use strcmp or check length instead. Also, this condition is redundant because empty strings are already avoided by logic above, but the comparison itself causes undefined behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Comparing string literals with != is invalid in C; should use strcmp or check length/first character instead. Also, after processing, ci is always null-terminated and non-empty when added, so this check is unnecessary and syntactically wrong.]
